section_title: Definition of joint stock company.-- (1)For the purposed of this part, so far as it relates to registration or companies
content:
355. Definition of joint stock company.-- (1)For the purposed of this part, so far as it relates to registration or companies
limited by shares, a joint-stock company means--
(a) a company having a permanent paid up or nominal share capital of fixed amount divided into shares, also fixed amount,
or held of and transferable as stock or divided and held partly in one way and partly in the other; and
(b) formed on the principle or having only for its members as the holders of those shares or that stock and for no other
person.
(2) Such a company, when registered with limited liability under this Act, shall be deemed to a a company limited by shares.
